mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing activity and capacity utilization that boosts corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when INDPRO is increasing, or Rank IC is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) trades below 0.25%, the zero lower bound environment suppresses short-term yields and pushes institutional capital into risk assets, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or the mean conditional return fails to exceed the risk-free rate.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in payroll employment (PAYEMS) alongside a decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals robust labor market tightening that raises aggregate wage income and consumer spending, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of realized instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power in a regression (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) predict negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when headline inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 2.5% annually, because overheated consumer demand in this regime hastens monetary tightening and compresses profit margins.
failure_condition: RSAFS acceleration coincides with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of high-inflation months, or the strategy Sharpe ratio is negative in this regime over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals strength in residential investment and construction employment that feeds into broad economic momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window, or the conditional mean return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) without an offsetting increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) compresses real discount rates and lifts nominal revenue growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change exceeds 0.5 percent while FEDFUNDS is unchanged or lower
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term carries a negative coefficient in a pooled regression.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) declining below 6.0% signals a tight labor market that increases wage pressure and reduces corporate profitability expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE below 6.0% coincides with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of months, or the mean return conditional on this trigger is positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Negative industrial production growth (INDPRO) combined with a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms a contractionary supply-demand loop that triggers risk-off equity outflows and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is negative and UNRATE 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the strategy alpha is statistically insignificant (t-stat < 1.5).
